meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: UN peacekeeper killed in southern Lebanon as Israeli invasion intensifies
items.summary: Indonesian peacekeeper has been confirmed killed in artillery fire as Israeli invasion of southern Lebanon intensifies.

items.title: ‘This is a nightmare scenario if it escalates further.’
items.summary: Elisabeth Kendall says the Houthi attack on Israel demonstrated a willingness to carry out their threats

items.title: Did Israel miscalculate in launching the war on Iran?
items.summary: Like in other conflicts, Israel launched the war on Iran together with the US without a realistic political plan.

items.title: Displaced families in Sudan struggle as aid falls short
items.summary: WFP aid disruption in Sudan’s White Nile State leaves thousands of displaced families cut off.

items.title: US-Israel war on Iran: What’s happening on day 31 of attacks
items.summary: While Trump has talked about diplomacy, fears of a ground invasion in Iran loom as US troops arrive in the region.

items.title: Mohamed Salah warned against US move as MLS clubs eye Egyptian footballer
items.summary: Egyptian official says Liverpool star will fade away if he opts for the MLS as San Diego FC owner welcomes compatriot.

items.title: Settlers burn vehicles in town near Hebron
items.summary: Video shows vehicles set on fire in the town of ad-Dhahiriya, near Hebron, during an attack by Israeli settlers.

items.title: Land Day: What happened in Palestine on March 30, 1976?
items.summary: Fifty years ago, six unarmed Palestinians were killed and over 100 were injured protesting Israel’s land confiscations.

items.title: By the 50th anniversary of Land Day, Palestinians lose most of their land
items.summary: Palestinians still face illegal Israeli settlement expansions, land seizures and restrictions on access to their land.

items.title: ‘We’re killing them, bro’: Israelis correct Iranian online streamer
items.summary: Iranian musician thanks young Israeli women for ‘saving’ Iran through US-Israeli air strikes.

items.title: Canadian YouTuber captures congestion of dozens of ships in Hormuz Strait
items.summary: A Canadian activist captured dozens of oil tankers and cargo ships crowding the vital waterway.

items.title: Trump says Iran’s ‘had regime change’ as he describes ‘boatloads of oil’
items.summary: US President Donald Trump says the US-Israeli war on Iran has effectively brought ‘regime change’.

items.title: Fakhar Zaman charged with ball‑tampering by Pakistan board in PSL
items.summary: Pakistan Super League has been jolted by ball-tampering accusation against Zaman, which allegedly occurred on Sunday.

items.title: Australia to halve fuel tax as global energy crisis deepens
items.summary: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese announces the tax cut as the price of crude oil tops $116 a barrel.

items.title: Amnesty warns of human rights risks at 2026 World Cup
items.summary: Amnesty International says millions of fans attending the 2026 World Cup in the US face serious human rights risks.

items.title: Marco Bezzecchi wins USA MotoGP to extend perfect start to season
items.summary: The Italian wins his fifth straight Grand Prix to take control of the world championship standings after three rounds.

items.title: CAF general secretary quits amid AFCON final controversy
items.summary: Veron Mosengo-Omba's resignation follows the fallout over the CAF board's decision to strip Senegal of the AFCON title.

items.title: IAEA says Iran’s Khondab heavy water reactor no longer operational
items.summary: The UN’s nuclear watchdog says Iran’s Khondab heavy water production plant is no longer operational.

items.title: Iranian academic describes US-Israeli attacks on Iran’s universities
items.summary: Doutaghi says the attack reflects a wider pattern and raises questions about what defines ‘legitimate retaliation'.

items.title: Netanyahu orders deeper Israeli invasion into Lebanon
items.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has ordered the military to expand its invasion in southern Lebanon.

items.title: Oil tops $116 a barrel as Iran accuses US of preparing invasion
items.summary: Crude prices continue to climb as world faces its biggest energy crisis in decades.

items.title: Iranian attack damages Kuwait power and desalination plant, kills worker
items.summary: Kuwaiti authorities say Indian worker killed in attack that also caused 'significant material damage' to building.

items.title: Iran war live: Trump says wants to take Iran’s oil; Kuwait power site hit
items.summary: Kuwait says an Indian worker has been killed in an Iranian raid as attacks across the Gulf continue.

items.title: How will the Houthis’ involvement shape the war?
items.summary: The Yemeni group has launched missiles towards Israel in support of Iran.

items.title: European nations criticise Israel’s death penalty plans
items.summary: France, Germany, Italy and the United Kingdom raise concern over 'de facto discriminatory character of the bill.'
